column1: Average annual expenditures and characteristics of all consumer units, Consumer Expenditure Surveys, 2021-2024
Number of consumer units (in thousands) a/
White, Asian, and all other races, not including Black or African-American
Hispanic or Latino origin of reference person:
At least one vehicle owned or leased
Food prepared by consumer unit on out of town trips
Maintenance, repairs, insurance, and other expenses
Residential phone service, VOIP, and phone cards
Vehicle rental, leases, licenses, and other charges
Audio and visual equipment and services
Pets, toys, hobbies, and playground equipment
Other entertainment supplies, equipment, and services
Social Security, private and government retirement
Interest, dividends, rental income, and other property income
Public assistance, Supplemental Security Income, Supplementary Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
Unemployment and workers' compensation, veterans' benefits, and regular contributions for support
Mortgage principal paid on owned property
Estimated market value of owned home
Estimated monthly rental value of owned home
a Data are rounded to the nearest thousands.
b Beginning with 2024 publication, estimates developed by TAXSIM models will no longer be published.
c Value is too small to display.
d Due to corrections that occurred post-publication, 2022 numbers are different than the 2022 tables published in September 2023.
e Due to corrections that occurred post-publication, 2023 numbers are different than the 2023 tables published in September 2024.
g Beginning with 2023 data, Gasoline and other fuels is a new expenditure category.

h Data have a Relative Standard Error (RSE) equal to or greater than 25 percent. These estimates should be used with caution. See www.bls.gov/cex/tables-getting-started-guide.htm#section5 for more information.
# Data collection for this item began in June 2020. Stimulus payments delivered to respondents interviewed in April and May of 2020 were not included in estimation.

Note: All values have been rounded, and as a result some cell values have been rounded to zero. This is particularly evident in the characteristic section. When data are not reported or are not applicable (i.e., missing values), tabulated cell values have been set to zero.
Source: Consumer Expenditure Surveys, U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, December, 2025
